title: A Kinder, Gentler Larry Ellison? Oracle Turns New Face to Its User Community
author: Katherine Jones (Aberdeen Group)
abstract: Aberdeen Insight by Katherine Jones covering Oracle Apps World San Diego (Apr 2002). Notes a shift in Oracle tone — from new-product strutting to installed-base upgrade focus on the e-Business 11i Suite, rapprochement with OAUG, rebirth of outsourcing (Oracle hosting with claimed 31-84% IT infrastructure savings), and a new customer-care role for Mark Barranchea. Written during the post-dot-com downturn that constrained new-license revenue.
importance_rationale: Captures Oracle's pivotal strategy shift toward upgrading the installed base and embracing application hosting during the post-dot-com contraction — a turning point later validated by Oracle Fusion and Oracle Cloud strategies.
relevance_rationale: The 11i upgrade cycle and Ellison-era outsourcing narrative are historical, but the 'customer-centric focus' pivot and hosted application pattern became dominant industry themes (SaaS, Oracle Cloud).
prescience_rationale: Accurately anticipated Oracle's long-run move to hosted/managed software (Oracle On Demand → Oracle Cloud); 'daily close' became standard ERP feature; outsourcing/BPO became mainstream.
